codeReview.summary: All five requirements are fully implemented and correct. Types, filtering logic, sort logic, and UI are all consistent. Build passes. Pagination reset includes the new search dependency. Edge cases (empty search, special characters) are handled correctly via String.includes() (no regex, no crash). One minor non-blocking observation about unnecessary optional chaining on non-optional fields.

issues.description: Optional chaining on `lead.ownerName?.toLowerCase()`, `lead.propertyAddress?.toLowerCase()`, and `lead.folioNumber?.toLowerCase()` is unnecessary — these three fields are typed as non-optional `string` in the Lead interface. At runtime the chain is safe (if somehow undefined, the whole expression short-circuits to undefined rather than crashing), so this is defensive but inconsistent with the interface. No behavioral impact.
`a.score ?? -Infinity` — `score` is typed as `number` (non-optional) in the Lead interface, so the nullish coalescing fallback is unnecessary. Harmless but noisy.

summary: Clean, complete implementation. All 12 expected behaviors from the feature spec (VAL-DASH-001 through VAL-DASH-010, VAL-DASH-043, VAL-DASH-044) are covered by the code. The search filter correctly handles case-insensitivity, empty-string restores all leads, and special characters are safe because String.includes() is used (not a regex). The three new sort modes use -Infinity for null values, putting them at the bottom as required. FilterState types, DEFAULT_FILTERS, isDefaultFilters, and the pagination reset dependency array are all updated consistently. No blocking issues.
